name: sca_baseline
description: SCA baseline — normal steady-state load with real CIS policy size (207 checks, ~5KB each). Reference run
notes: Reference scenario: establishes the RSS/CPU/latency baseline for SCA before stress runs.
Payload validated: 14.490 docs indexed correctly in wazuh-states-sca, compliance as arrays,
rules as list, ~4.9KB/doc. Zero indexer_error. sca_check_template.json accepted by mapping.
Key differences from baseline.json:
- payload_kind: sca_check → uses sca_check_template.json (~5KB real vs ~1.6KB synthetic).
- data_size: 207 — matches the real CIS Ubuntu 22.04 policy (207 checks).
Real payload captured from Ubuntu 5x agent (see sample_payloads/sca_check_template.json).
- session_delay: 10s — reduces (not eliminates) the intra_session_race.
Known limitation — intra_session_race:
WorkersQueue uses hardware_concurrency() workers. On machines with >8 cores (ref: 4),
End message can be dispatched before all 207 DataValues finish writing to RocksDB.
GapSet detects gaps → ReqRet → sender marks session failed.
Observed: 20-core machine → ~52% reqret rate regardless of session_delay.
This is a known manager issue (see intra_session_race.json). NOT a payload problem.
What to read from this scenario:
- rss_growth_mb: primary metric — observed ~195-228 MB per 120s run with 10 agents.
- disk.inventory_sync growth: ~12-18 MB (RocksDB during send phase).
- end_ack latency p50: ~6s (indexer processing time for 207 checks x 5KB).
- logs: all zero — no indexer errors, no session timeouts.
Run this FIRST before any stress scenario to get RSS/CPU/disk reference values.
